titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 609

div.p: William king of Scots to all worthy men of his whole land, clerk and lay, greeting: Know that the dispute which was between the church of Durham and the church of Crowland about the toun of Edrom and the church of the same toun and all its pertinents has been concluded in my court in the presence of me and of my bishops and worthy men, so that the toun and church of Edrom with all its chapels and pertinents passes to the church of Durham in perpetuity. As witnesses: Richard the bishop of St Andrews, Ingram the bishop of Glasgow, Nicholas the chancellor, John the abbot of Kelso, the Earl Waldef, Richard de Morville the constable, Walter son of Alan, Dapifer [Steward], David Olifard [Oliphant]. At Perth.
